title: Ansible AWX Inventory Sync Failure and Host Unreachable Trending
app: AWX external logging integration, Splunk HTTP Event Collector
spl:
index=iac (sourcetype="ansible:awx" OR sourcetype="ansible:result")
| eval inv_fail=if(sourcetype="ansible:awx" AND match(lower(status),"(?i)failed|error"),1,0)
| eval unreachable_evt=if(sourcetype="ansible:result" AND unreachable>0,1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(inv_fail) as inventory_sync_failures, sum(unreachable_evt) as unreachable_runs, dc(inventory_name) as inventories_touched by _time, organization
| eventstats median(unreachable_runs) as med_unreach by organization
| where inventory_sync_failures > 0 OR unreachable_runs > med_unreach * 3
| sort -inventory_sync_failures
description: Stale inventories cause automation to target the wrong hosts or skip new systems, while rising unreachable counts flag VPN, firewall, or credential regressions across fleets. Combining AWX inventory job status with play outcomes surfaces controller-side versus host-side problems.
value: Protects patch coverage and compliance scans from silent shrinkage when cloud or CMDB feeds break.
implementation: Replace `baseline_unreach` with a subsearch or `eventstats` median over 7d. Ensure `inventory_name` and `organization` exist on AWX events. For `ansible:result`, aggregate unreachable per hour per org.
visualization: Dual-metric timechart, table (inventory × last error), map or breakdown by cloud account if available.
grandmaExplanation: We watch infrastructure-as-code runs and policy so changes to systems stay visible and governed.
knownFalsePositives: Transient errors during platform maintenance, network blips, or certificate renewals on shared services.
